mission.title: coredns: Add private DNS support for azure plugin
mission.description:
### 1. Why is this pull request needed and what does it do?
Fixes a bug in `azure` plugin introduced during refactor

### 2. Which issues (if any) are related?
closes https://github.com/coredns/coredns/issues/3113

### 3. Which documentation changes (if any) need to be made?
Updated the readme

### 4. Does this introduce a backward incompatible change or deprecation?
Yes, changes the syntax for `azure` plugin block

resolution.summary:
[ Quoting <notifications@github.com> in "Re: [coredns/coredns] Add private D..." ]
>```
>    azure resource_group_foo:example.org resource_group_foo:example.private {
>      tenant 123abc-123abc-123abc-123abc
>      client 123abc-123abc-123abc-234xyz
>      subscription 123abc-123abc-123abc-563abc
>      secret mysecret
>      access both
>    }
>```

yes some like this, I would remove the `access` and just have `private` and `public` as
keywords. Thus:

```
    azure resource_group_foo:example.org resource_group_foo:example.private {
      tenant 123abc-123abc-123abc-123abc
      client 123abc-123abc-123abc-234xyz
      subscription 123abc-123abc-123abc-563abc
      secret mysecret
      public
      private
    }
```

And default to `public` or so.
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
